problems.title: Why did the universe begin in a state so exceptionally suited to the growth of i
problems.summary: Why did the universe begin in a state so exceptionally suited to the growth of irreversible structure and knowledge, and can that specialness be explained without smuggling in teleology or anthropic s
problems.full_text: Why did the universe begin in a state so exceptionally suited to the growth of irreversible structure and knowledge, and can that specialness be explained without smuggling in teleology or anthropic selection? This is the deepest unresolved question inherited by the conjecture: it relocates the arrow-of-time problem to the origin and measure of low-entropy initial conditions, but does not yet explain whether such conditions are dynamically selected, typical in a larger cosmology, or brute facts.

problems.title: Can Born-rule probabilities be derived from the conditions for stable, reproduci
problems.summary: Can Born-rule probabilities be derived from the conditions for stable, reproducible record formation rather than assumed? The conjecture suggests probabilities reflect how often branches become durabl
problems.full_text: Can Born-rule probabilities be derived from the conditions for stable, reproducible record formation rather than assumed? The conjecture suggests probabilities reflect how often branches become durable records, but this raises a major open question: why should the frequencies of record stabilization match the squared amplitudes specifically, instead of some other measure over branches? Turning this into a testable derivation would be a significant new problem.
